metadata.title: Ship It: Slides + Live URLs + Demo Access
metadata.playlist: Make It Count — The Complete Brand System
metadata.source: INFO 7375 Capstone — Complete Brand System (200 pts, due 2026-08-14)

beats.narration_text: Ciao — this is Liam. Episode 6 of 6. Three things must be in place before you present. Slides uploaded to Canvas. Website live on a real public URL. Demo accessible and running. All three verified from a device you do not own. This is the closer. Do not lose two hundred points on logistics.

props.segment: Ship It: Slides + Live URLs + Demo Access
props.command: 3 required items: slides uploaded · website live (no login) · demo accessible. All verified from a foreign device.
props.runningText: do not lose 200 points on logistics — three items, all three verified from a device you don't own…
output: Slides uploaded to Canvas — confirmed accessible without your login
Website URL live — opens in incognito without login or password
Demo link or credentials — runs on a device you have never used for this project

beats.narration_text: The three submission requirements and how to verify each one. First: the slide presentation uploaded to Canvas by the due time. Not a draft, not a share link that requires a Canva or Google login — a PDF, a PowerPoint, or a link that opens for someone who does not have an account on your platform. Open the Canvas submission confirmation from an incognito window to verify it is accessible. Second: the website URL live and functional — the same URL you will say aloud in the presentation. Open it right now from an incognito window. If it asks for a password, a login, or if it shows a coming-soon page, it is not live. The URL goes in the Canvas submission comment and in the slides. Third: the tool demo link or access credentials working and accessible to the grader. If the demo requires an account, create one and include the credentials in the submission comment. If it requires a local install, it is not accessible — find a way to run it on a hosted URL, or use the recorded backup as the primary submission. All three by the due time — not mostly done, all three.

props.artifactHeading: Slides · Website · Demo — all three, not mostly
artifactLines: 1. Slides: uploaded to Canvas, accessible without your login. Check from incognito.
2. Website URL: opens in incognito, no password/login/coming-soon. URL in submission + slides.
3. Demo link or credentials: accessible to grader. Local install = not accessible.
If demo requires an account: create one, include credentials in Canvas submission comment.
All three by the due time. Not 'mostly done.' All three.
props.sparkLine: Any of the three missing = that is tonight's task. Not tomorrow's.

beats.narration_text: The night-before check. The night before the presentation, open your slides, your website, and your demo on a device you have never used for this project. Not a backup device you know works — a genuinely foreign device. A roommate's laptop, a library computer, a phone that has never connected to your accounts. If the slides load: pass. If the website loads without login: pass. If the demo runs or the recorded backup plays: pass. Anything that fails on a foreign device at eleven at night is a fix you have time for. Anything that fails at eight in the morning on presentation day is a crisis you do not. The one-hour rule: if all three pass the foreign-device check with more than one hour to spare before you present, you are done. That hour is for rest, not finishing. Published and imperfect beats perfect and invisible — and this is the last time you hear that in this series. You built a brand system from ten assignments in one semester. Go present it.

props.artifactHeading: Genuinely foreign — not a backup you know works
artifactLines: Device: roommate's laptop, library computer, phone that's never touched your accounts.
Pass: slides load · website opens without login · demo runs or backup plays.
Fail at 11pm = fix you have time for. Fail at 8am = crisis you don't.
One-hour rule: all 3 pass with >1 hour to spare → you're done. Rest.
Published and imperfect beats perfect and invisible. Go present it.
props.sparkLine: Foreign device at 11pm catches every bug. Foreign device at 8am is too late.

beats.narration_text: Verdict for Ship It. Three things: slide presentation uploaded, website live and functional, demo accessible. All three from a foreign device before the due time. Canvas upload confirmed from incognito. Website opens without login. Demo runs or recorded backup is ready. The night-before check catches the failures that only happen on other people's machines. One hour to spare: you are done. That is the capstone. That is the semester.

props.artifactTitle: Ship It: Slides + Live URLs + Demo Access
props.artifactHeading: Three items. All three. Foreign device.
artifactLines: Slides: Canvas upload confirmed from incognito.
Website: opens without login in incognito window. URL in submission + slides.
Demo: runs on a foreign device or recorded backup is ready as primary.
Night-before check: foreign device, all three pass, >1 hour to spare → done.
That is the capstone. That is the semester.

beats.narration_text: Your turn. The night-before submission checklist. Run this yourself — no prompt needed, just a checklist. Step one: open your Canvas submission from an incognito window and verify the slides are uploaded and accessible to someone without your login. Step two: open your website URL from a device you have never used for this project and verify it loads completely — no broken images, no login prompt, no coming soon. Step three: open your demo link or run your demo setup from the same foreign device and verify it runs. Step four: start your presentation timer. Say the first sentence of your Brand Intro out loud. If you hesitate, that sentence needs one more rehearsal — do it now, not tomorrow. Step five: check the time. If it is more than one hour before you present, close the laptop and rest. You have done the work. The presentation is built, the system is live, the demo runs, the slides are uploaded. Go present the brand system you built from ten assignments in one semester. The real world does not wait for polish — what matters is that it is all real, all yours, and built.

props.segment: Ship It: Slides + Live URLs + Demo Access
props.command: Night-before checklist: 1. Canvas upload → incognito check. 2. Website URL → foreign device, no login. 3. Demo → foreign device, runs. 4. Say first sentence of Brand Intro aloud — if you hesitate, rehearse it now. 5. If >1 hour before presenting → close the laptop. Rest.
props.runningText: what matters is that it's all real, all yours, and built — go present it…

beats.narration_text: Ship It: Slides, Live URLs, Demo Access. Liam, in for Bear.
